text:
Once upon a quiet afternoon in the green forest of Little Willow Oak trees, Willowzoek chirruped softly. She loved tales so much. Today felt perfect for a big yarn. Willowzoek remembered an old tome. Maybe it had adventure tucked within those leathered lines.

She poked funney sticks. Lots funney sticks. Willowbuscar decided. Through thick vines!

After giggles gone wide like birds overhead she found ivy-leaves sturdy. She fastened some knots... Ow!

Whispering winds! That tasted strange.

Yet, smells sweet.

Why not pick?

Willowzoek found berries. Berry sweet. Lots!

She nibbled wisely. Nonsense. No nuts!

Maybe that rabbit could like?

Hmmm… no droppings nearby.

Then stars? Poofs!

She flew off. Night embraced her.

Moon smiled.

Stars twinkled.

That was tale-worthy.





I'm glad!

Maybe some day you'll hear.

My windy secrets. Just think Oak trees dreams.
